name: Kaj Obst
text:
Finkenwerder II U-båtsbunker är en betydelsefull historisk struktur belägen i Hamburg, Tyskland. Byggd mellan 1941 och 1943, representerar denna massiva betongkonstruktion en viktig del av 1900-talets militära arkitektur.

Bunkern utformades för att erbjuda skydd för fartyg och utrustning. Dess strategiska placering vid floden Elbe gav enkel tillgång till Nordsjön. Den imponerande konstruktionen, med sina tjocka väggar och tak, demonstrerar dåtidens avancerade ingenjörskonst.

Inuti bunkern fanns utrymmen för flera fartyg samtidigt, tillsammans med verkstäder, lager och personalutrymmen. Denna mångsidiga design möjliggjorde effektivt underhåll och logistik under en utmanande period.

Efter kriget stod strukturen kvar trots försök till rivning, vilket vittnar om dess robusta konstruktion. Idag fungerar Finkenwerder II som ett historiskt landmärke som lockar besökare intresserade av arkitektur och 1900-talets historia.

Bunkern väcker viktiga frågor om bevarandet av historiska platser och deras betydelse för vårt kollektiva minne. Den erbjuder en unik inblick i en komplex historisk period och fungerar som ett pedagogiskt verktyg för att förstå den tidens tekniska framsteg och samhälleliga utmaningar.
textTranslated:
The Finkenwerder II U-boat Bunker is a significant historical structure located in Hamburg, Germany. Built between 1941 and 1943, this massive concrete structure represents an important part of 20th century military architecture.

The bunker was designed to offer protection for ships and equipment. Its strategic location on the Elbe River provided easy access to the North Sea. The impressive construction, with its thick walls and roof, demonstrates the advanced engineering of the time.

Inside the bunker were spaces for several ships at once, along with workshops, warehouses and staff quarters. This versatile design enabled efficient maintenance and logistics during a challenging period.

After the war, the structure stood despite attempts at demolition, testifying to its robust construction. Today, Finkenwerder II serves as a historical landmark that attracts visitors interested in architecture and 20th century history.

The Bunker raises important questions about the preservation of historic sites and their importance to our collective memory. It offers a unique insight into a complex historical period and serves as an educational tool for understanding the technological advances and societal challenges of the time.
title: Fink II U-Boot-Bunker

name: Lutz Mueller
text: Sehr beeindruckender historischer Ort, um sich einmal die Monstrosität und Größe eines solchen ehemaligen U-Boot Bunkers zu vergegenwärtigen. Im zweiten Weltkrieg als Teil der Deutschen Werft zum Schutz der empfindlichen U-Boote vor Luftangriffen gebaut, wurde dieses Gebäude nach dem Zweiten Weltkrieg gesprengt. Heute erinnerern nur noch diese gewaltigen Ruinen an die dunkle Geschichte dieses Ortes. denn in unmittelbarer Nähe befand sich auch ein Außenlager des KZ Neuengamme, dessen Häftlinge hier zur Zwangsarbeit eingesetzt wurden und die bei Kriegsende fast alle auf Todesmärschen ums Leben kamen. Das sollte man sich auch immer im Angesicht dieser beeindruckenden technischen Leistung ins Gedächtnis rufen.
textTranslated: Very impressive historical place to visualize the monstrosity and size of such a former submarine bunker. Built during World War II as part of the German Shipyard to protect vulnerable submarines from air raids, this building was blown up after World War II. Today only these huge ruins remain as a reminder of the dark history of this place. Because in the immediate vicinity there was also a subcamp of the Neuengamme concentration camp, whose prisoners were used here for forced labor and who almost all died on death marches at the end of the war. This is something that should always be kept in mind when faced with this impressive technical achievement.
title: Fink II U-Boot-Bunker

An information board describes the history of the place. A short walk to the observation tower on the banks of the Elbe is also worthwhile. The "Rüschpark" ferry pier on line 62 is nearby. Coming from Teufelsbrücke/Finkenwerder.

Wer einen Eindruck von der ursprünglichen Grösse des Bunkers haben möchte, schaue sich den Eintrag U Boot Bunker Valentin in Bremen an.
textTranslated: A spooky place. The foundations make the enormity of the horror clear. It is right next to the Airbus runway and so you can see planes landing here with a spectacular perspective. If you would like to get an impression of the original size of the bunker, take a look at the entry U Boat Bunker Valentin in Bremen.
